patternsFound.analysis: CommentForm correctly handles create-only mode without edit confusion
patternsFound.reasoning: No edit mode implemented, so no create/edit confusion possible

patternsFound.analysis: Create-only modal with no edit functionality
patternsFound.reasoning: Wizard pattern for creation only, no edit mode to cause confusion

patternsFound.analysis: CRITICAL: Same pattern as draft bug - mode prop not consumed in save logic

details.problem: Mode prop set but PostCreator always calls createDraft
details.issue: PostCreator ignores mode prop in save logic

riskIndicators: Modal passes edit data but wrapped component doesn't use it properly
Save handler doesn't check edit mode
Single save handler for both create and edit

patternClasses.description: Modal components that accept edit props but child components ignore edit context
Components that receive mode props but don't use them in business logic
One save function for both create and edit operations without mode checking

codebaseRisks.risk: High probability of edit/create confusion in modal wrappers
codebaseRisks.recommendation: Audit all modal components that accept edit props

codebaseRisks.risk: Single save handlers may not distinguish between create/edit modes
codebaseRisks.recommendation: Review save logic in all form components for mode awareness

codebaseRisks.risk: Hooks provide both create and update methods but components may not use correctly
codebaseRisks.recommendation: Ensure hooks are used appropriately based on component context

proactiveFixRecommendations.fix: Implement mode-aware save logic in PostCreator.saveDraft
Add validation that mode props are consumed by wrapped components
Implement TypeScript checks for mode prop consumption
